_metadata.description: Explicit reviewed exemptions for active pairwise interaction rules that must remain visible without a numeric high-dose threshold.
_metadata.purpose: Reviewed exemptions for active pairwise rules whose clinical copy mentions high-dose use but must not be suppressed by a numeric app gate.

pairwise_interaction_ids.DSI_WAR_VITK: Warfarin has a narrow therapeutic index; the safety action is consistent vitamin K exposure and INR review, not suppression below a supplement-dose floor.
pairwise_interaction_ids.DSI_WAR_VITE: Warfarin anticoagulation is individualized by INR and concurrent bleeding risks, so this interaction remains present rather than being hidden by a vitamin E dose gate.
pairwise_interaction_ids.DSI_LEVOTHYROXINE_SOY: The actionable issue is absorption timing for supplemental soy isoflavones; high-dose wording only adds follow-up monitoring and does not define a safe no-action floor.
pairwise_interaction_ids.DSI_ANTICONV_FOLATE: Folate management with anticonvulsants depends on seizure history, medication, and pregnancy context; a generic numeric floor cannot safely suppress clinician-review guidance.
pairwise_interaction_ids.SSI_ZINC_COPPER: Copper depletion reflects prolonged cumulative zinc exposure and baseline copper status, which a one-day label dose cannot fully model; retain the non-suppressed monitoring signal.
pairwise_interaction_ids.SSI_KAVA_ACETAMINOPHEN: This is a major shared-hepatotoxicity concern. It must remain visible regardless of an incomplete or low apparent supplement dose.
pairwise_interaction_ids.DSI_THYROID_TURMERIC: The relevant action is levothyroxine separation and thyroid monitoring; the high-dose phrase is advisory and does not establish a verified safe suppression threshold.
pairwise_interaction_ids.DSI_VITE_ANTICOAG: Anticoagulant risk is medication- and patient-specific. This major interaction must remain visible rather than being suppressed by a generic vitamin E threshold.
